mappings.rationale: Explains how loads flow through beams, frames, and shells in A.7.b (structural systems), with detail on wood, masonry, concrete, and steel construction in A.6 and bridge design in B.3.
The definitive introduction to B (civil engineering works), addressing the design and construction of dams (B.1), bridges (B.3), tunnels (B.4), harbour works (B.5), and lighthouses (B.6).
Explores how architectural vision translates into buildable form, directly relevant to A.1 (preconstruction planning and design) and the interplay between aesthetics and construction technology.
Traces how reinforced and prestressed concrete (A.3.i) and steel-frame construction (A.6.c-d) enabled the radical forms of twentieth-century buildings.
Provides the materials-science foundation for A.3 (building materials: cement, ceramics, glass, metals, concrete, plastics) and A.4 (testing of building materials).
Addresses how buildings fit into larger urban systems, essential for understanding A.1 (preconstruction planning and design programming) and infrastructure integration.
Examines site design and the integration of buildings with surrounding landscapes, directly relevant to A.1 (preconstruction planning).
Essential for understanding A.8 (acoustics and sound-control techniques), explaining how sound behaves in enclosed spaces and how buildings manage noise.
Geological conditions determine foundation requirements (A.7.a) and the suitability of stone as a building material (A.3.e), making this a key reference for site assessment.
Surveying grew out of navigational science, and this volume illuminates A.2 (surveying procedures) and the precision techniques used to lay out building foundations.
Traces the evolution of construction techniques (A.6) and building components (A.7) across centuries of British architectural development, from medieval timber to Georgian masonry.
A broad introduction to the engineering principles that underpin both A (general building construction) and B (civil engineering works), connecting theory to practice.
